question: What are the cell-level energy densities of prototype solid-state batteries in 2026?
ground_truth_facts: Sulfide prototype cells reach cell-level energy densities of 480 Wh/kg to 510 Wh/kg.
This is roughly double the density of high-performance lithium-ion cells.
EV range increases by 60-80% allowing over 700 miles per charge.

question: What are the comparative costs of solid-state vs lithium-ion battery packs in 2026?
ground_truth_facts: Solid-state battery packs cost approximately $300/kWh in 2026.
Lithium Iron Phosphate (LFP) pack costs have stabilized at $80/kWh to $85/kWh.
Solid-state batteries are not expected to reach cost parity with lithium-ion until 2030.

question: What are the environmental and safety risks of sulfide-based solid-state battery electrolytes?
ground_truth_facts: Sulfide electrolytes react with ambient moisture to release highly toxic hydrogen sulfide gas.
H2S gas is both toxic and flammable, posing recycling risks.
Standard hydrometallurgical recycling facilities cannot process sulfide batteries without upgrades.
